title: linanthus androsaceus subsp. luteolus
intro: Linanthus androsaceus subsp. luteolus, also known as Luteolus or Yellow Rock Penstemon, is a rare, yellow-flowered plant that belongs to the Polemoniaceae family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Traditionally, Luteolus is prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, which can be used to treat various conditions, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues.
